description: Promotion ledger for NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5. Source of truth for ledger-derivable rules; the preset's compiled_rules block is the compiled output of this file. Entries are typed-prefix + 3-digit counter (e.g., nist-tomap-001).

entries.checklist_item: Map block_type=TableOfContents → element_type=reference and set semantic_role=toc_entry. Field-level enrichment (label, target_page, dot_leader) is a separate rule.
entries.closure_notes: Closed at ledger_only_single_extract scope: the applier reproduces the snapshot script's effect against a single PDF extraction. Full pipeline closure (preset+core only, no snapshot domain rules) is gated on B.3 retiring the snapshot rules; fixture provisional flag flips when that lands.

candidates_that_could_disprove: actual:p14:block:8 — text='Table of Contents' with source_type=Caption (toc_heading), NOT a TOC entry. Rule must not match (source_type filter rejects it).

Any future non-NIST PDF where block_type=TableOfContents should map to a different element_type. None known at v6 authoring; this is the candidate for migration to a shared baseline ledger once a counterexample appears.
evidence_and_intent.deterministic_rule: If source_type='TableOfContents' → set type='reference' and semantic_role='toc_entry'. Per-element field enrichment (label, target_page, dot_leader) is handled by a separate text_classifier_rule entry; structural grouping under a toc parent is a separate structural_grouping_rule entry.
evidence_and_intent.lives_in: nist_preset for now; rule_class=baseline_adapter signals candidacy for migration to a shared baseline ledger once a second PDF confirms universality.
evidence_and_intent.observed_pattern: pdf_oxide emits block_type='TableOfContents' for every line of the NIST 800-53r5 TOC on page 14 (38 elements). Without a mapping, type stays at 'unknown_region' and downstream queries that filter by type=reference miss every TOC entry.

entries.added_by: agent:claude-opus-4-7 (B.2 bulk authoring after nist-tomap-004 proof)
entries.checklist_item: Parse TOC dot-leader text into label / target_page / dot_leader extras.

candidates_that_could_disprove: A TOC without dot leaders (some books use space-fill); rule won't match — safe.
Page numbers in the TOC that aren't trailing digits — rule won't match — safe.
evidence_and_intent.deterministic_rule: If source_type='TableOfContents' AND text contains a run of >=4 dots with a label on the left and a positive integer on the right → set label=<left>, target_page=int(<right>), dot_leader=true. Failures (no dot leader, non-numeric tail) are silent — rule does not fire.
evidence_and_intent.lives_in: nist_preset (universal TOC-with-dot-leader pattern; could migrate to baseline).
evidence_and_intent.observed_pattern: After nist-tomap-004 maps TableOfContents → reference, 38 entries still lack label/target_page/dot_leader semantic fields. The text pattern is 'LABEL ......... PAGE' (dot leader between).

do_not_apply_if: text does not match the dot-leader pattern

entries.human_reviewed_by: human:graham (B.2 batch — dot-leader regex universal for any TOC)

entries.added_by: agent:claude-opus-4-7 (B.2 bulk authoring after nist-tomap-004 proof)

candidates_that_could_disprove: A Caption that doesn't match the prefix pattern — rule won't match — safe.
evidence_and_intent.deterministic_rule: If source_type='Caption' AND text starts (case-insensitive) with 'TABLE' → set semantic_role='table_caption'. Specificity ordering (equals_ci > prefix_ci) ensures nist-semantic-004 ('table of contents' equality) fires first and claims the toc_heading element BEFORE this prefix rule.
evidence_and_intent.observed_pattern: NIST 800-53r5 table captions all begin with 'TABLE' (e.g. 'TABLE C-3 (AU-7(2)) Title').

guards.counterexample_rationale_exempt: Narrow scope: applies only when both source_type=Caption AND the text matches the specific prefix regex.

entries.added_by: agent:claude-opus-4-7 (B.2 bulk authoring after nist-tomap-004 proof)

candidates_that_could_disprove: A Caption that doesn't match the prefix pattern — rule won't match — safe.
evidence_and_intent.deterministic_rule: If source_type='Caption' AND text starts (case-insensitive) with 'FIGURE' → set semantic_role='figure_caption'.

guards.counterexample_rationale_exempt: Narrow scope: applies only when both source_type=Caption AND the text matches the specific prefix regex.

entries.added_by: agent:claude-opus-4-7 (B.2 bulk authoring after nist-tomap-004 proof)
entries.checklist_item: Caption with text='Table of Contents' → type=section_heading + semantic_role=toc_heading.

candidates_that_could_disprove: A Caption that doesn't match the prefix pattern — rule won't match — safe.
evidence_and_intent.deterministic_rule: If source_type='Caption' AND text equals (case-insensitive, trimmed) 'Table of Contents' → set type='section_heading' and semantic_role='toc_heading'. equals_ci has highest specificity priority, so this rule fires before any prefix_ci rule that would also match.
evidence_and_intent.observed_pattern: One Caption element ('Table of Contents') is the TOC's own heading.

guards.counterexample_rationale_exempt: Narrow scope: applies only when both source_type=Caption AND the text matches the specific prefix regex.

entries.release_conflict_waiver_rationale: nist-tomap-010 (block_type_map: Caption → caption) sets type=caption as the default for ALL Caption elements. This rule is more specific: when the Caption's text equals 'Table of Contents' (case-insensitive), the element is the TOC heading, not a generic caption — type must be section_heading. The waiver acknowledges this intentional override of a block_type_map default by a more specific text_classifier_rule.

entries.added_by: agent:claude-opus-4-7 (B.2 bulk authoring after nist-tomap-004 proof)
entries.checklist_item: Group consecutive toc_entry elements under a synthetic 'toc' parent with heading lookahead.

candidates_that_could_disprove: A TOC under 3 entries (rule.min_run_length); not grouped — safe.
Sparse toc_entry markers from a misclassified Body block; would not be 3 consecutive — safe.
evidence_and_intent.deterministic_rule: Find runs of >= 3 consecutive elements with semantic_role='toc_entry'; synthesize a parent element with type='toc', page_range, entry_count, and heading={element_id, label} from the immediately-prior element if its text matches /^Table of Contents$/i. Set parent_id on every leaf.
evidence_and_intent.lives_in: nist_preset (NIST TOC layout; could generalize to other dot-leader TOCs).
evidence_and_intent.observed_pattern: After nist-tomap-004 + nist-semantic-001, TOC leaves have type=reference + semantic_role=toc_entry. The baseline groups them under a synthetic 'toc' parent with the 'Table of Contents' caption as the parent's heading.

guards.counterexample_rationale_exempt: Structural rule with min_run_length guard; cannot over-apply to scattered elements.

entries.checklist_item: extract_tables should populate per-cell bbox in raw.rows[*].cells[*].bbox so downstream bbox refinement can shrink an over-broad table.bbox to the union of its cell bboxes.

core_issue_spec.b5_dependency: B.5 cannot author a working applier rule_kind=bbox_refinement_rule until Rust exposes cell bboxes OR a separate table_postprocessor is built to coordinate classify_blocks + extract_tables.
core_issue_spec.defect_description: pdf_oxide.extract_tables emits row_count/col_count and a coarse table.bbox but does NOT expose per-cell bboxes in the raw output. On NIST 800-53r5 the coarse bbox is over-broad (occupies most of the page horizontally). With cell bboxes available, a deterministic post-processor could shrink table.bbox to union(cell.bbox). Today this signal is unavailable; the workaround would involve coordinating classify_blocks (text positions) with extract_tables, which belongs in a shared table_postprocessor layer rather than the ledger.

spot_check_findings.alternative_signal_available: doc.classify_blocks() within the table bbox returns ~24 text blocks per page (header row + body rows). Each text block has a real bbox. A post-processor could union those text-block bboxes to compute the tight table bbox WITHOUT a Rust change — but it would require classify_blocks + extract_tables coordination, which is naturally a table_postprocessor concern.
spot_check_findings.conclusion: 27 candidates route to pdf_oxide_core (core_component=table_extractor) for cell-bbox-exposure fix; B.5 also needs a downstream bbox-shrink rule once cell bboxes are available.
spot_check_findings.raw_table_bbox_observation: All three tables emit page-space bbox starting at x≈89.76, w=432.0 (occupies most of page horizontally), with row_count/col_count populated. rows[i] is a dict, NOT a list-of-cells with bboxes.

candidates_that_could_disprove: A table where the raw bbox correctly equals the cell-union (i.e., NOT over-broad). Such tables should not trigger the post-processor rule.
A non-NIST table fixture where cell bboxes are already exposed — would prove the gap is NIST-layout-specific, not core. Spot-check on a second PDF in B.4.1.
evidence_and_intent.deterministic_rule: extract_tables should expose per-cell bbox in the raw output (e.g., raw.rows[i].cells[j].bbox). With cell bboxes available, a post-processor rule (preset OR core invariant) can set table.bbox = union(cell.bbox) when the raw bbox is materially larger than the union (defining 'materially larger' as a tolerance the rule owns).
evidence_and_intent.lives_in: pdf_oxide_core (Rust src/tables/) — the cell-bbox-exposure gap is a core API completeness issue, not a NIST-specific transform.
evidence_and_intent.observed_pattern: All 27 NIST over-broad tables share the same shape: pdf_oxide.extract_tables emits a table.bbox covering most of the page, rows[i] are dicts (not lists of cells with bboxes), and no per-cell bbox field is exposed in the raw output. Spot-checked p19/p467/p490: each has row_count/col_count populated but `cells with bbox: 0`.

guards.counterexample_rationale_exempt: core_pending: rule is not active until Rust patch lands. Counterexample requirement applies to ledger-applied rules; this entry is a core_issue_spec marker.

entries.lessons_for_second_pass_agent: Future bbox_over_broad classifications should compute union(cell.bbox) before reporting over_broad. If the cells span the same area as the raw bbox, the table is NOT over-broad — it just covers a large region. Visual proportion to page size is not a reliable signal.
entries.rejection_rationale: B.5 deep spot-check on p19/p467/p490/p456 revealed that the raw table.bbox IS ALREADY EQUAL to union(cell.bbox) with 0.00pt drift across all 4 samples. The 104/172/140/172 cells in those tables each carry a populated bbox (accessed via rows[i].cells[j].bbox, NOT via the top-level cells field which doesn't exist). The second-pass agent's bbox_error_class='over_broad' label was a false positive — based on visual inspection ('table covers most of the page') rather than data inspection. No Rust patch is needed for these 27 candidates. They reclassify to positive_fixture.

entries.checklist_item: Single paragraph_block on p1 has an over-broad bbox; separate from the 27 table cases.

core_issue_spec.b5_dependency: Independent of the 27-table issue. Could be resolved by an applier bbox_refinement_rule if a deterministic signal exists, OR by Rust block_classifier change.
core_issue_spec.defect_description: One paragraph_block on p1 has an over-broad bbox. Likely a block_classifier edge case (merged paragraphs or page-region misclassified as a single block).

candidates_that_could_disprove: Any well-formed paragraph_block — should NOT trigger the same defect.
evidence_and_intent.deterministic_rule: Needs separate investigation — block_classifier may be merging multiple paragraphs or emitting a page-region as one block. Spot-check before authoring a Rust fix.
evidence_and_intent.observed_pattern: actual:p1:block:6: a paragraph_block element with bbox occupying most of the page. Different shape from the 27 table cases.

guards.counterexample_rationale_exempt: Single-candidate outlier; investigate before authoring rule.

entries.added_by: agent:claude-opus-4-7 (GS001 R4 — row 2: side DOI watermark → header_footer_noise per WebGPT plan)
entries.checklist_item: Route NIST 800-53r5 left-margin DOI watermark text ('This publication is available free of charge from: https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5') to type=header_footer_noise. Uses bbox_constraints.x_min_lt=0.10 (left page edge) AND a regex text anchor — so normal body content cannot be captured even if a body paragraph happened to mention 'this publication is available free of charge'. category=semantic_enrichment so it fires AFTER type_remap (Body→paragraph_block, List→list). release_conflict_waiver=true authorizes overwriting the prior type field; a `release_conflict_waiver_override` event is recorded for audit.

second_pass_results: GS001 R3 golden_slice_comparison: row 2 (side_watermark_or_page_chrome_noise) missing because blocks 3/4/5 typed as paragraph_block/list
WebGPT 2026-05-13 R3 verdict APPROVE_EVIDENCE: 'Row 2: nist_preset_ledger. NIST-specific side DOI/page chrome. Use text anchors plus left-margin/page-edge geometry'

candidates_that_prove: actual:p27:block:3 (text 'This publication is available free of charge from:', type=paragraph_block, bbox.x_min=0.0344)
candidates_that_could_disprove: Any NIST page where a body paragraph genuinely contains 'This publication is available free of charge' as content. The bbox_constraints.x_min_lt=0.10 guard prevents body text (which starts at x_min ≈ 0.147) from matching.

extract.regex: ^This publication is available free of charge

entries.closed_by: agent:claude-opus-4-7 (GS001 R4 — pinned by regression test tests/test_nist_page_28_regression.py::test_side_doi_chrome_routed_to_header_footer_noise)
